prompt:
RE-RENDER the cortex 'how a brain works' deck to FIX the brand-cold failure. This closes the taste loop: the cortex deck is the worked example that PRODUCED David's colour rule — now it must be the first deck to PASS it.

AUTHORITY — read these first, in order, and treat the doc as guidance to apply with JUDGMENT (it is NOT a maximisation target):
  (1) docs/david-design-patterns.md — focus on TWO sections: 'Colour as Brand vs Colour as Data — the two-layer model (2026-06-10)' AND the top 'CORRECTION (2026-06-09) — diagrams are SPARING, not the default'.
  (2) tools/design-lint/RUBRIC.md — the operational pass/flag extract you will be linted against.

WHAT IS WRONG NOW (David's own diagnosis, 2026-06-10): the live designs (02/03/04/06 on :7422, dated Jun 9) are BRAND-COLD — cool semantic (green/blue/purple) was made LOAD-BEARING on CONTENT cards (region tiles, memory-kind tiles) and the warm brand anchor — yellow #ffde59 especially — was DROPPED. Verified on disk: 0 occurrences of #ffde59 across all four. This is the predictable overshoot from the v3 ticket (q-20260609-cortex-brain-v3) which WRONGLY ordered 'DROP the yellow #ffde59, use green/blue/purple as block colour'. That instruction is the bug. REVERSE IT.

THE FIX (the two-layer model, applied):
  - WARM brand is LOAD-BEARING. Every screen keeps >=1 bright warm anchor: yellow #ffde59 + cream #faf5ec/#f0ebe4 + brown #342d2d. Content the viewer reads as SUBSTANCE (the memory-region tiles, the memory-kind cards — these ARE the point, not diagram nodes) wants WARM brand emphasis, not a category colour.
  - COOL semantic (green/blue/purple/teal) is a SPARING ACCENT, licensed ONLY on genuine STRUCTURE: an ordered stage in a flow, a node/legend key in a real diagram. NEVER as the dominant fill on content tiles.
  - amber #c8841a ONLY for 01/02/03 numbered sequences; never as fill or text-on-tint. Never warm-on-matching-tint (no orange/yellow text on brown/beige).
  - RESTRAINT is the master rule: mostly-neutral ground (cream/grey) + sparing accent. Colour must NEVER overpower. Fewer colours used meaningfully.

Apply BOTH tests from the doc to every coloured element: (1) ROLE test — is it structure (cool ok) or content (warm)? (2) DOMINANCE test — is warm still load-bearing with >=1 yellow anchor on the screen?

DO NOT REPEAT v3's SECOND overshoot: do NOT force every design into an SVG diagram. HTML is the default and the strength. Keep a diagram ONLY where the content genuinely IS a flow/cycle/topology and it clarifies — 04-brain-sleeps (cycle) and 02-brain-remembers (flow) earn a SPARING diagram with THIN NEUTRAL connectors (colour in blocks, not lines) + a legend; 03-four-memories (a taxonomy) and 06-brain-anatomy stay HTML-primary. Do not convert working HTML layouts wholesale to SVG.

SCOPE: the four cortex designs in the EXISTING .mochaccino/ workspace — 02-brain-remembers, 03-four-memories, 04-brain-sleeps, 06-brain-anatomy. DATA-FIRST/MVC: edit data/NN-*.json where the fix is data (trim dense prose to one short blurb per element; add ghost-text + warm-anchor intent), edit designs/NN-*/index.html for the colour/treatment; index.html fetches its data, never inlines. KEEP provenance meta.source = cortex path + commit_sha a129a5d50ba8a140c288b6f2eb03ddadcee873a9. Leave 01-session-journey and 05-dark-factory untouched. The server is ALREADY serving :7422 — regenerate files in place, do NOT start a new port. curl each of the four + designs/index.html over http to verify 200 before reporting.

REPORT ONE line: the gallery URL + the 4 design names + for each, confirm 'warm anchor restored (>=1 #ffde59), cool semantic demoted to structure-only'. Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (write the run record, move ticket to done/, write the reports/ handback — DO NOT skip the reports/ handback).

args.label: cortex-brain-v4
args.recipe: two-layer-colour (warm brand load-bearing + cool semantic structure-only) + restraint + sparing-diagrams + restore-yellow-anchor
args.fixes: reverses v3 brand-cold (drop-yellow) AND v3 force-diagram overshoots
